stem: The function above models the height h, in feet, of an object above the ground t seconds after being thrown straight up in the air. What does the number 48 represent in the function?

answerOptions.content: The maximum height, in feet, of the object
The maximum speed, in feet per second, of the object
The initial height, in feet, of the object
The initial speed, in feet per second, of the object

rationale: Choice C is correct. The value of h(0) = 48 represents the initial height of the object, the value when t = 0 seconds. Choices A and B refer to maximum values, which would require more analysis. Choice D is the initial velocity, but 48 is height, not speed.
